title: Personal Blog & Portfolio
description: This very site — a personal blog and portfolio built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript.
content:
# Personal Blog & Portfolio

This is the source of the site you are looking at right now.

## Stack

Built with Next.js 16, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, and Bun as the runtime.

## Features

- Blog with individual post pages
- Resume with career timeline
- Portfolio showcase with project tiles
- Static generation for all content pages

title: Smart Grid Interface — BEAM v1
description: First generation of Bodil's in-home energy platform — the bridge between a constrained ESP32 device and a cloud backend that can simulate and act on its data.
content:
# Smart Grid Interface — BEAM v1

Scaffold — to be expanded.

The first generation of Bodil's platform: getting reliable telemetry and control off constrained ESP32 hardware and into a backend that could simulate and act on it.

## Device & firmware

ESP32 (ESP-IDF) connectivity stack — Bluetooth provisioning, WiFi and LTE/NB-IoT fallback, and peer-to-peer device communication — plus the firmware-side parsers, protocol layer and machine interface.

## Backend

A data aggregator and a server-side state holder that keep the database, the Python energy-simulation engine and the field devices consistent, exposed through a TypeScript/Node API.

## Role

Systems Engineer at Bodil Energy (Nov 2022 – Jun 2024). See the Resume page for the full context.

title: Distributed System Orchestration for Flexibility Market — BEAM v2
description: Second generation of Bodil's platform — taking the device from prototype to product and orchestrating a fleet that bids into grid-balancing (FCR-D) in real time.
content:
# Distributed System Orchestration for Flexibility Market — BEAM v2

Scaffold — to be expanded.

Building on BEAM v1, the second generation took the prototype to a shippable product and connected the device fleet to real flexibility markets — shifting the focus from a single device to orchestrating a responsive fleet.

## Product transition

Defined hardware, firmware and software requirements and coordinated the hardware design; shipped Over-the-Air (OTA) firmware updates and on-device power/energy metering.

## Data & orchestration

A scalable, multithreaded data-collection system in Rust over MQTT for high-frequency real-time field data, and a service-manager engine on AWS and GCP (TypeScript, Bun, Docker, Kubernetes) tuned for responsiveness and availability under grid-balancing signals.

## Market integration

Integrated the IBM/Andel Flex platform to participate in the FCR-D frequency-regulation market.

## Role

Hardware & Software Engineer at Bodil Energy (Jun 2024 – Jun 2025). See the Resume page for the full context.
